methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Drones & Autonomous. Range: $6.0B - $10.0B.

private.name: Joby Aviation
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Public), post-money $10B+

data.narrative:
Archer Aviation, a public-stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector, currently trades at a $5B valuation following $1.5B+ in total capital raised. The peer universe consists of six private and public companies in autonomous logistics and urban air mobility, with Joby Aviation, Zipline, and Nuro serving as the closest comparables by sector and valuation tier.

A peer-median approach was applied across three same-sector comparables. Joby Aviation (public, $10.0B), Zipline (Series G, $7.6B), and Nuro (Series G, $6.0B) establish a peer median of $7.6B with a range spanning $6.0B to $10.0B. This method is the primary applicable triangulation technique given the limited set of mature, disclosed valuations in this nascent sector.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $6.5B to $8.7B, with a central estimate of $7.6B. Archer's current $5B valuation sits approximately 34% below the peer median, a discount potentially attributable to stage-specific factors or market repricing. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the peer set—all three comparables operate in adjacent but distinct subsectors (urban air mobility versus autonomous delivery)—and the limited depth of public comparables, which constrains alternative methodologies. Revenue multiples and path-to-profitability data were unavailable, preventing income-based cross-checks.
